name: Pentastarch
description: Pentastarch is an artificial colloid (hydroxyethyl starch derivative). Pentastarch is a subgroup of hydroxyethyl starch, with five hydroxyethyl groups out of each 11 hydroxyls, giving it approximately 50% hydroxyethylation. This compares with tetrastarch at 40% and hetastarch at 70% hydroxyethylation, respectively. It is sold under the name Pentaspan by Bristol-Myers Squibb and is used for fluid resuscitation. It is considered a plasma expander because it remains primarily intravascular after infusion.
indication: The primary intended use of pentastarch is as a substitute for older colloids such as albumin or hetastarch for use in plasma volume expansion.
